mapping.a_element: Gaussian excitation making the Hermite basis orthogonal so the diagonal rule is exact
mapping.b_element: Gaussian-field assumption yielding the disconnected ('Gaussian') part of the covariance

mapping.a_element: off-diagonal Gram terms / 3rd-4th cumulants that perturb the Gaussian-exact result
mapping.b_element: effective-area overlap and Poisson (discreteness) self-pair contributions beyond the smooth Gaussian term

mapping.a_element: comparison against a brute-force / bootstrap reference to validate the analytic penalty
mapping.b_element: comparison against a brute-force exact covariance for sparse samples

brief_justification: The only real overlap is the generic 'Gaussian baseline plus corrections' pattern: A computes the exact excess-L2 RISK (a bias-like mismatch penalty) of an orthogonal-polynomial projection estimator via a moment quadratic form and Hankel-Cholesky factorizations, whereas B computes the COVARIANCE (error bars) of a pseudo-C_l power-spectrum estimator via harmonic mode-coupling (Narrow-Kernel Approximation) plus shot noise. Different estimands and different governing mathematics mean no single transferable mechanism maps role-for-role between them.
